channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. — 19 replies — 76.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump.

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-10 20:34 UTC

OP

I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. I've lost so much money since then, I can't believe I actually fell for it. I'm still mostly in just BTC but I actually got scammed, feels horrible man.

Early replies (first 3)

62335000
>buying when it's on the news

62335010
>>62335000
trips of wisdom

62335027
>>62334973
First of all ure gambler so u deserve to lose everything. Second of all, you never buy on the news - you invest (youre gambling not investing so this does not concern you) before news happen by using public info and calculating how good or bad news are plausible.
Third of all, next time look THEY WAY THEY SHOOK HANDS retard. Did this sergey cuckoldery look to you like Trump gave a flying shit about him? This was a sell signal if anything

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335038
>>62334973
>few thousand dollars
>so much money
Pick one

62336812
>>62334973

You should have bought Polkadot

62340148
>>62334973
Chin up lad

62340299
>>62334973
You are waiting on the banks releasing their products ahead of the Clarity Act. There were two laws that needed to be passed before the value of those laws can be demonstrated, Genius was the first. Clarity is next, maybe this month. After that, banks can finally start using smart contracts in production environments and also the Tokenized real world asset market will explode.

If these two markets are everything you hear about in the news and Link's token price is still in the dumps, then you can panic. Link's token price was always suppressed to keep it out of the top 10. They're not going to let it rip till they have to since they don't scrubs like us owning a share of their financial network.

62341169
Buy the rumor.
Sell the news.

62342097
>>62334973
brock > misty

62342174
>>62340299
You say that, but we could buy less if the price was higher

62342183
>>62340299
this is larpcope to keep bagholders from killing themselves

62344770
>>62342174
this

>>62342183
and this

/biz/ can't handle being wrong about link, and the delusion will only spiral deeper

62344773
>>62342097
Disagree. Brock has a major type weakness against Misty.

62346469
>>62342174
>You say that, but we could buy less if the price was higher

This presumes people understand the Link token. The banking world and VCs are aware that a bunch of racist frogs are all obsessed with the project and they overlook such a thing. The common masses, on the other hand, they only pay attention to the top 10. It's impossible to shakeout every frog, but they're doing a great enough job keep Link under the radar otherwise and keeping people too demoralized to even recommend the project as an investment to others.

62346485
>>62346469
>da elites don't want a bunch of anons to make 3 million dollars!
holy shit you cuckolds are really insane lol

62346620
>>62346469
Baggies will really stare you dead in the eye and parrot that tired fucking line about the elites suppressing price on purpose to shake them out as boomers and normie retards are getting spoonfed in semis and AI. Evil disingenuous nigger. I really can't wait for North Korea to target CLL with Mythos, I really can't.

62346674
>>62346620
How else do you explain the convenient BTC dumps every time there's big news about Chainlink? How do you justify the DTCC saying they're launching their own chain with Link as the infrastructure and the price doesn't move? The DTCC processes over a quadrillion dollars in transactions a year. How else do you explain this?

62346725
Should've bought NANO (XNO)
and enjoyed movie

62347714
>>62334973
I bought my first 2k LINK immediately after seeing an American Psycho business card meme about it. I had no idea what it was and hadn't even heard of Ethereum at that point, it just resonated with me for some reason.
Then after reading the white paper and doing some basic research I bought another 10k or so.

item.title: ICP — 49 replies — 37.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-06-10 19:26 UTC

OP

>we have steve jobs at home

Early replies (first 3)

62336952
>>62336934
soon

62337106
>>62336934
ICP has the best tech

62337150
>>62337106
ICUP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337787
>>62336934
Dis-lui que je lui passe le bonjour

62337830
>>62337760
That’s a good mindset. Worst case scenario you lose a few thousand dollars or you make half a million when the AI alt season starts.

62337832
>>62337385
I understand the futures Sam Barkman scam. I was going off a $200 top. Even $80 top puts ICP down 97% however

62337836
>>62337760
10k is a good gamble for something that has potential to retire you, just don’t over extend because the risk is big. ICP is imo still the most exciting narrative besides chainlink in crypto right now

62337916
>>62337836
Chainlink is not that exciting lol

62337929
>>62336934
Lol the black turtleneck lives on

62337937
>>62337760
For your own good, don't, trust me, i've been suckered into this scam for 6 years, it has fundamental issues stemming from it's core team, they are ok with the price hovering low just surviving and extracting

62337958
>>62337787
are you talking about mm?

62338026
>>62337937
I’ve held on and off for years as well. But seems like things are starting to click now.

62338066
>>62338026
Don’t listen to him, he’s a known fudder on biz, he hated ICP for years, his obsession with moonman is a mental illness.

62338597
>>62337916
Chainlink is very exciting, they research so much

62338901
>>62336934

62339051
>>62336934
>his first name on his handle is in lower caps
Woah these zoomers are so young and hip. I bet he's like 21 or something.

62339665
please fuck my ass

62339717
>>62337372
Looks like the entire top 30 is in that same list. What the fuck are you doing.

62340134
>>62339717
Dfinity is the most important because they have a partnership with the government of Pakistan a country with 100 million people.

62341606
>>62336934
.

62342451
>>62338066
will we moon this year?

62343082
>>62342451
Yes brother. Jerry is the OG

62343093
>>62337365
>1. ICP was launched 5 years ago not 5

62343103
>>62337568
>ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You are Indian

62343169
>>62342451
what holding ICP does to a mf

62343888
>>62336934
This is how Dom see himself in his own mind.

62343892
>>62343169
jerry did so many shrooms that he is taking out loans against his family and soul to buy the increasingly lower bottoms

62344871
i need it

62345073
>>62342451
I watched this guy on youtube around 2017 on crypto, how is he doing now?

62346259
>>62343093
Fucking lmao.

62347287
>>62337372
WOOOOOOOW IT SIGNED WITH THE UN???? let me check the price real quick LMAO

62347366
>>62347287
Buy the dip!

item.title: It can't possibly take another leg down, RIGHT?! — 9 replies — 36.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It can't possibly take another leg down, RIGHT?!

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-10 20:37 UTC

OP

It can't possibly take another leg down, RIGHT?!

Early replies (first 3)

62347004
I've got cash ready to push into the market.

62347011
>>62346926
40k bottom my nigguh.

We gotta get to it one way or anoda

62347015
>>62347011
>muh cycles
Bitcoin will never be below 60k ever again.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347019
>>62346926
Z - E- R- O

62347028
Of course it's gonna go down. Everyone is about to get rekt with SpaceX, normies are either about to lose a lot or distrated with ball games, Iran is BTFOing the anglozionist empire and way to many people were excited about their slop Index gains.

62347189
>>62347028
>slop index gains
A beautiful and important notion has just been discovered

62347196
>>62346926
bobos... is it that time again?

62347703
Bitcoin will never be above 60k ever again.

62347728
Another leg down to 50k in june. Bounce and crab in july-august. Last leg down to 35-40k in september-october.

Simple as

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 28 replies — 26.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-06-10 19:41 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339835
>>62339829
100%
And I caught him red handed

And Chainlinkies be all like: "Durrr imagine caring this isn't important"

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

62340978
>>62339781
>words words words
It's funny how you're immediately identifiable by the spergy seethe that radiates from your posts.

Even funnier, >>62339844 posting the madmen meme.
>"We don't think about Linkies at all!!"
>Op literally creates a thread ranting about Chainlink because some other random thread that doesn't mention Chainlink could have been made by a Linkie

62340989
>>62340978
>TLDR linkjeet seethe

didnt read lol

62340992
BOOOOOOOOOOM

BULLISH AT XRP

62341232
Bumping this bullsih thread
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62342495
>>62339768
>My retarded shitposts made someone so mad he made an expose thread on me
FXCKING BULLISH KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62343674
>>62339768
>>62339781
>>62339835
>>62340989
>linkcucks and xrpaypigs having a retarded biz slapfight as they both go to 0

62344317
KABOOOOOOOOM>>62339768

62344795
>>62339781
This. I don't hold either but the link spam is so fucking stupid and annoying. They got decimated in a war length campaign of threads and saw zero wins and now it is over and over with this same shitty failed meme that ironically got funny when XRP posters just did it back to them to mock them. I have zero dog in this race but XRP is the clear winner in the messaging war.

62344908
>>62344795
>t. the same person a different id
manufacturing consensus on a dead board is pretty cringe desu senpai

62345267
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM
link

62345354
Except XRP is actually being used

62347256
KABOOOOOOOOM

62347334
>>62344795
BOOOOOOOOOOM YOU HOLD XRP

62347434
>>62339781
>”I don’t care”
>paragraph about how much he cares

62347439
>>62339768
Both LINK and XRP are gay

item.title: BTC is not a good buy anymore — 8 replies — 14.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC is not a good buy anymore

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-10 20:10 UTC

OP

Everything in crypto has pumped 6,000,000%

no way to make easy money anymore

let it all crash and burn

Early replies (first 3)

62343855
>>62343850
Notice how the anti Christianity shills never suggest a better option? They always just shit on Christianity and tell you you're wrong yet can never muster up a worthy replacement. It is because they know there is no other. Christ is King. They just want you to be a pathetic nihilist athiest loser and to give up any hope. Better yet you become a blasphemous larpagan and invite some horrific diety into your life. They don't actually care about you at all, just evil creatures trying to destroy you, your hopes and your dream.

62343857
>>62343855
Wait..

Notice how the anti Bitcoin shills never suggest a better option? They always just shit on Bitcoin and tell you you're wrong yet can never muster up a worthy replacement. It is because they know there is no other. Bitcoin is King. They just want you to be a pathetic nihilist sidelined loser and to give up any hope. Better yet you become a blasphemous stock fag and invite some horrific 8% YoY returns into your life. They don't actually care about you at all, just evil creatures trying to destroy you, your hopes and your dream.

62344093
>>62343857
>>62343855
the bulls have lost their minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62344385
>>62343855
AI god on earth governing everything

62344538
>>62344093
>pattern recognition between glowie spammers
I can just see the matrix code

62344788
>>62343850
Keep repeating this for another 6 months, then it'll be time to buy.
Damn, thank you, anon.
I know I can't beat smart money with their geniuses, supercomputers, politicians, lobbies and privileged information.
The only reason why I make money is people like you.
So thank you.

62347550
>>62343850
saylorcoin is fucked till hes out

62347593
>>62344788
might as well continue the meme with 2025 and 2026
>120k
>I'm an idiot
>69k
>LOL I aint buying that shit

>>62335674
Original blocks aren't relevant to XRP's consensus you retarded nigger. Not every network is proof of work/stake. item.title: BTC — 5 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-10 20:15 UTC

OP

Did anyone figure out why the value seems to be cyclical?
Whats the best exchange and wallet for a new to investing anon?

Early replies (first 3)

62347134
>>62347031
so they can continuously pump and dump and pretend it's simply "natural" and "mathematical" and whatever other bullshit marketing they do

62347139
>>62347031
>cycles
Human psychology

>exchange
Kraken

>wallet
Your memory, but I’ll let other people shill their products below

62347162
>>62347139
He's new to it. Start with the Bluewallet app on your phone, look for more secure alternatives as you go.

https://bitcoin.org/en/choose-your-wallet

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347465
Thank you for your helpful responses frens

62347618
>>62347031
Its a self fulfilling prophecy at this point but it started because of the halving.

item.title: Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting is next week. — 6 replies — 6.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting is next week.

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-10 19:48 UTC

OP

Kevin Warsh's first FOMC meeting is next week.
His definition of inflation does not include price increases like that of oil caused by the Iran conflict.
He has also hinted at using projected US AI production to determine current policies.
I'm leaning towards him cutting rates, but then again, the meeting is on Nigger Wednesday. We'll just have to see!

Early replies (first 3)

62347212
>>62347208
the market has priced in hikes, so a cut would have quite a remarkable effect

62347277
>>62347208
This might the most low IQ brown post I've ever read in a while, surpassing the retardation of posts of those retards that keep hoping Link will ever go above $15 again.

Honest question, do you live in a cave or something? I'd understand that, because you probably hasn't read ANYTHING or even saw ANYTHING related when he did his FOMC minutes.

Anyway, how does it feel to be either retarded or ignorant and post on a business board?

62347284
>>62347277
I'd understand that, because you probably haven't read *

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62347324
>>62347208
He only has 1 vote out of 12

62347448
>>62347208
Warsh will hold rates steady for now

62347476
>>62347277
Other than insiders, no one knows what's going to happen.

62345936
>>62345931
Fair. Monero or privacy crypto could have been world changing, except you can never cash out or buy anything with it

62345958
>>62345936
By design.

62345960
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62345970
most of the so called utility coins have been around for 14-15 years, if they were actually going to do something they would have 10 times already. Even the fucking dotcom boom crashed and recovered in half the time. What else is there to wait for? Clarity Act? Don't make me laugh.

62346026
>>62345970
You could have held Walmart and apple for 20 years before it really took off, 12 years for Amazon…so you’re a retard

62346045
>>62345960
Does nothing for the xrp price

62346051
>>62345960
>30+ partners

62347125
>>62346045
I’ve been holding XRP for years, but no single good news has pumped its prices. Even when XRP got clarity in court, its price only went up measly 40 cents before it dumped back down. You can declare XRP intergalactic bridge currency, but it will still dump or crab. This is because they’re using bots to suppress the price, which has been documented for a long time.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 189 replies — 33.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 189 • Updated: 2026-06-10 15:04 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

62340953
>>62340347
>But his timing is fully transparent.


classic boyscout bold faced lie

there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales other than them doing it 4 times per year. often it will be completely different months as years past.

didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol

62340971
>>62340953
>there is nothing transparent about the team's token sales
- always around 7% of total supply annually
- always on Fridays
- always within 3 to 4 month intervals

62342184
>>62340953
>didnt they sell right before 10/10/25 lol
Those are the transactions I posted that >>62340971 is trying to brush off as "part of the plan"

He's moving the goal post even when this discussion just bolsters my point about the link token price not accurately reflecting network usage. I initially wasn't thinking of the "routine token unlocks" so I'll give you my thanks.

62342243
>>62342184
You're claiming he unlocked right before the 10/10 crash to "capitalize on market movements".

The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval. Like he's done for years.

62342296
>>62342243
Oh no no no, he most certainly did unlock just before the crash. It was part of the panic and it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis.

62342402
>>62342296
>he most certainly did unlock just before the crash
Who said he didn't?

>it most certainly wasn't "luck" when even you admit it's a 3-4 month basis
It was luck BECAUSE he'd already been selling on a 3-4 month basis for years at that point.

62342424
Reminder you must only say positive things, otherwise you are a shill.

62342440
>>62342424
say something nice about chainlink

62342501
>>62313866
>>62313871
Glad to see some of us are still here... I didn't do the presale but t0 Binance buyer.
It's absolutely grim the state of this project.

62342517
>>62342243
>The reality is he unlocked on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.

yeah they do it once a quarter
this particular one we're talking about just happened to be directly before one of the largest selloffs in industry history

>but duuuuuuuuude he always does it like that

no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months. so no it is not just like previous years.

62342559
>>62342517
>no, in years past that quarter's unlock happened in completely different months
That's because he's unlocking every 3 to 4 months. Not in the same month every time.
He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.

62342602
>dumps straight on the most shady CEX are "unlocks"
peak doublespeak

62344923
>>62342602
All of Chainlink's tokens go through Binance, including node payments.
Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

62346060
>>62342559
>He even unlocks almost at the exact same time of day.


not sure why you keep repeating this as if this implies some transparency.

>duuuuuuuuuude just hang out on the blockchain every friday and you'll see it coming

its ok to concede they don't want to be transparent here. if they did they would just post a set schedule.

>>62344923
>Meaning they're not dumps, they're unlocks.

no one falls for this outside of the biggest retard boyscouts
where do the coins go?

62346092
>>62346060
Unlocking the same amount annually for years, within the same interval for years, and even around the same time of day for years implies transparency, yes.

>where do the coins go?
among others to the nodes

62346134
link blowz

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 57 replies — 21.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 57 • Updated: 2026-06-10 18:03 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62343037
>>62342700
kill yourself you stupid fucking r*ddit spacing bot.

62343068
all you fags are fags.

Blue collar work is important also. Sure wiring can be done pre-fab for new construction, but here's a fun fact: 100% of all the houses that have already been built have already been built.
That means that their cast iron pipes are about to collapse, or their pvc pipes are about to come unglued or crack. It means that if they want to redo the kitchen, someone has to come tear out the old cabinets and install new ones and install new light fixtures and move the gas line and put a heated tile floor in.

It means that the HVAC company you hire to do that has equipment that breaks, and when you shove baby wipes in the toilet and it clog, you have to hire gus to come snake that shit out.

I have a friend who's son is slightly autist and apprenticed as a welder at age 15. At 16, he got hired by a company to be the shop welder for the summer: $35/hr, 40 hrs a week. That's $70k a year, but remember, he was only 16.

Anyway, you can live a good life on $100k if:
You buy a small house and do your own repairs instead of having to hire some other dildo to do it.
Get a 6 year old Tacoma and drive it for 20 years

Once you have those costs under control, you should have lots of spare money to invest.

62343110
>>62342700
>child labor

62343166
>>62343110
Not really. Better than him not doing shit all summer. He already has to take 5 days off.

62343167
>>62342798
>da booooooots
Kek

62343175
>>62343166
child labor is worse than not doing shit all summer. At that age he should study, fuck and do drugs instead of adopting the goy mentality

62343255
>>62343175
There's absolutely zero things wrong with child labor when it involves doing normal ass jobs adults do with the same standards.

People always jump to kids being kept as slaves or beaten or made to carry explosives or work in some dangerous factory without training and it's stupid.

There's zero reason why a 12 year old kid shouldn't be alloweded to get paid to wipe down tables at a McDonald's or bus tables at Applebee's over his summer.

62343264
>>62342700
Had a "white collar" semi-richfag come to me one time for help. He was scared to death to open the radiator cap so he could add water and I told him how to check by pressing the upper hose to see whether there was any pressure or not. Come to find out he didn't even know where to add more water, he didn't know what the radiator cap was or how to open it but was adamant about how dangerous it was so he wouldn't do it. How do these people drive a car?

62343298
>>62342700
What happens when you sit on ifunny and read too many monarchtrump posts, gay ass op get off the board

62343302
>>62343264
they live comfortable lives and any discomforts can be solved with money

62343318
>>62343255
Your lack of education is showing.

62343323
Daily autistic browncel thread

62343328
>>62342700
Stop making these awful fucking threads

62343411
>>62343318
You are a fucking redditor.
>>62343328
Shut up.

62344022
>>62342700
I work a blue collar job.
No overtime
Air conditioned factory
I get a nice comfy chair
Basically watch a CNC machine all day.
Will be bumped up to $139,000 in 2027.
Feels good.

62344029
I guess im technically a blue collar? I work in a factory kinda supervising trades but I dont really do anything stuff myself. 250k tho.

62344036
The elites in power want the actual workers keeping civilization alive poor because they know what is to come.
>sit in a office all day? 300k!!
>create and fix everything? 40k and you should make less!
Always destroy anything you can when fixing these fuckers houses.

62344177
>>62344022
But that’s not even middle class.

62344183
>>62344029
Yeah, still blue collar unfortunately.

62344186
>>62344036
Not really, a fourth worlder can come into usa and do the job.

62344274
>>62342735
I think it's funny

62344383
>>62344274
My threads are funny? Wtf do you mean? What is so funny about my threads?

62344425
fuck trade school. anyone smart should learn to vibe code an app that uses crypto.

62344611
>>62342770
Those people are ether:
1. Retired
2. Trust fund leeches
Now you might say, “nuh uh, they are not parasitic blood sucking invertebrates”
And to that I would say, yes they are.
Money has to be spread out even to the non contributing members and often times instead of focusing on growing their portion; they spend energy on stealing it from the producing members.
About 1/4 of every family is productive.
About 1/2 are ambitious (not profitable)
And 4/4 are living luxuriously
That formula produces 1 producer, 2 layabouts and 1 thief; on average, in every generation.
Basically somebody has to be making the money wether it’s a company ran by the one ambitious and smart family member or my tax dollars in which case you are only advocating for leeching off of somebody and floating the idea it should be me and most people on this board.
So please fuck off and kys, immediately
Please

62344720
You know biz is in dire straits when it's gone the way of /ck/ with so many bot threads to generate a false sense of activity.... Remember to sage these threads

62344811
>>62342770
How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money? Like 10-20k might sound like " a lot of money", but even if you aren't rich thats like a year or two of savings, how is that shit going to last you your entire life?

62344833
You won't make it as a blue collar wagie but that doesn't mean its a dead end

Open your own business and build, in the end youre the boss of blue collar wagies and make more than 100k, blue collar work is high in demand and always will, someone has to build the physical shit and it aont be KI

62346901
>>62342700
never change, anon. I enjoy your little gay larps. i'm your fan, you're funny as fuck, I wish we had a biz party somewhere, you'd be the soul of the party unironically speaking

62346960
>>62346901
What larp?

62346974
>>62344611
>trust fund leeches.
Kek. Seething working class.
>>62344811
> How poorfag coded are you people that you think just maxing out a couple of credit cards is a lot of money
Not poor but maxing out one credit card is a lot of money.

item.title: Day 1741 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 9 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Day 1741 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-10 20:07 UTC

OP

Day 1741 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

62345698
this bot is gonna die from stack overflow before bitcoin ever reaches $1M lol

62345817
cucked deceptive fuck rope yourself or use the truthful count

62345888
>>62345698
I can count pretty high

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62345902
>>62345888
damn so you're not a bot, dont recall seeing you reply before. I forgive you.

62345906
>>62345902
>I forgive you
shit I meant to say sorry, lmao freudian slip perhaps?!!!!!!111111111

62346396
>>62345682

62346771
>>62345902
I haven't been very active in replying lately, but every thread is just me

62346932
>>62345682
What are the best ways to buy/store btc?

62347568
>>62346932
>buy
strike
>store
cold wallet, probably Trezor 3

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 46 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-10 17:17 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62341032
>>62339417
you've been conned, the fact that you think eth have any utility is a clear sign of that

62341036
>>62339328
Sold at the top. No regrets. will buy back in after saylor dumps his load

62342205
>>62339401
And why would you buy this?

62342982
>>62339417
I don't think it's fair to say nobody uses Bitcoin. It's just that Bitcoin's dominant use case ended up being different from what many people originally expected.

62344142
>>62339452
They're just blind about it

62344890
Is it good to buy now?

62344894
>>62339328
if you didn't sell it when it was over 100k it was not the right move

62345001
>>62344890
Anon, if you believe in BTC long term, buying is the easy part. Holding is harder. And now there are even ways to put BTC to work through native staking without giving up self-custody. Do with that information what you will.

62345004
>>62339462
What's the problem with that?

62345048
>>62344894
You can buy the dip ser

62345050
>>62341036
Buy eth and sol too

62345054
>>62340066
What's happening in October?

62345074
>>62345048
yea but you could have bought the 50% discount with some of the profits instead of fresh shekels

62345096
>>62345074
Smart guy

62345100
>>62345001
How can we stake btc?

62345127
hold till $1M

62345299
>>62345054
were supposed to bottom, 4 year cycle or some shit.

62345600
>>62345100
You haven't heard of Babylon? Native BTC staking already exists there. DYOR anon.

62345604
>>62339510
The real challenge is whether August actually lets you buy lower. Markets have a habit of humbling everyone's timelines.

62345772
>>62345299
Is 4year cycle even real?

62345775
>>62345127
40 years from now

62345776
>>62339328
I sold a small amount at 110k last year, bought a car. Unironically I felt bad once it started pumping back to 120k. Now my car has holded it's value better than BTC.

62345783
>>62345776
Car price depreciated. Nothing special

62345787
>>62341032
I just want x1000 . Utility is nothing

62345800
>>62345600
centralised services doesn't really give me a good impression

62345806
When to sell?

62345827
>>62339328
>Btc
>Eth
>Hbar
>Neo
>Etc
>Kava
>Rose
>Inj

62346229
>>62344890
Yep. Make sure you ignore the fiat value. If it falls to zero, BTC is a new way of finance independent from the outdated monetary system. Be grateful you are part of the revolution. I too will soon enter BTC paradise. Any day now...

62346754
>>62345800
Anon, where did I say anything about centralized services? You're arguing against something I never said. Read up on their TBVs and maybe it'll click.

62346765
>>62339730
That's understandable. It's hard to judge past decisions based on outcomes we couldn't have known at the time.
